themes.exampleQuote: A perfect speakeasy vibe with the best cocktails I've had in Pittsburgh.
Alicia was one of the best bartenders I've had, definitely deserves the bar manager position.
A superb riff on a Sazerac/Manhattan/old fashioned, slightly sweet, little liquorice flavor, booze heavy.
After we ordered a third round, we were informed we had to leave because there is a 90-minute time limit, only mentioned in the menu in very small print.
I have tried to get in a few times but it is always super packed.

notable_quote: I will be making the drive from Cleveland just for this bar in the near future.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently praise the moody botanical aesthetic, named bartenders, and inventive cocktails, but a poorly communicated 90-minute table-turn policy has produced the sharpest negative feedback Commerce Bar has on the books.
quarter_narrative: Commerce Bar spent Spring 2026 doing what a lot of quietly beloved East Liberty spots do: filling up early, turning tables on a timer, and generating real word of mouth without doing much to capture it. The Instagram account posted once in thirty days and published zero Reels, which means the bar's strongest visual asset, a candlelit, chandelier-hung room that reviewers keep calling 'moody,' 'vintage,' and 'botanical,' exists almost entirely in other people's camera rolls. No Pittsburgh TikTok coverage has been indexed for the business at all, so that entire discovery channel is currently closed. Across the Pittsburgh Bars family, Hidden Harbor holds the top rank on the strength of social momentum, the precise signal Commerce Bar is weakest on. Closing that gap starts with the room itself: it photographs well, the reviews confirm it, and nothing is stopping someone from pointing a phone at it consistently.
tldr_read: Strong reviews and a room that earns destination trips from Cleveland, but near-zero Instagram activity and no TikTok footprint keep Commerce Bar at Neighborhood Staple, ranked second in Pittsburgh Bars.
tldr_meaning: The sentiment floor is solid and creator fit is already at the family median, so the rank ceiling lifts the moment the bar's own feed starts reflecting what its regulars are already saying.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Second In Pittsburgh Bars, Built On Word Of Mouth. The Feed Has Not Caught Up.

playbook.headline: Post one Reel before next weekend
playbook.action: Zero Reels in 30 days while the room is visually strong enough to draw out-of-state visitors; one atmospheric Reel changes the discovery calculus immediately.

playbook.headline: Add phone and website to Google profile
playbook.action: Commerce Bar has no phone number and no website listed on Google, leaving the conversion path at half the family median and cutting off a basic decision-making step for new visitors.

playbook.headline: Address the time-limit policy publicly
playbook.action: One review specifically describes being blindsided by the 90-minute policy after ordering a third round; a single owner response and a clearer front-of-house disclosure would stop that thread from becoming a recurring sentiment drag.
